note: All 13 case(s) below were published directly to TestRail outside this repo's /fetch-section -> /migrate-section pipeline and were never tracked locally until this backfill (2026-07-23). Case IDs and creation dates indicate they predate this migration project. Content is taken verbatim (HTML converted to plain text only) -- no v2 style-rewrite, Plan Gates extraction check, or Suite 6 source_case_ids cross-reference has been done yet; grill_status is honestly marked not-tracked-by-repo rather than assumed confirmed. live TestRail section is named "Alert banners" (lowercase b) vs the doc's "Alert Banners".

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the Resend Verification Email link sends a new verification email
cases.preconditions: User's email address is not yet verified.

steps.expected: A banner "Check the [email] mailbox to verify your email address." with a "Resend Verification Email" link should be displayed.
A success toast should appear and a new verification email should be sent to the registered address.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the onboarding installation alert banner links to the banner installation page
cases.preconditions: Cookie banner not installed and no scan completed yet (onboarding pending).

steps.content: Verify the "You are almost there!" installation alert banner.
steps.expected: The banner should be displayed with the body "It's easy! Just copy and paste the code on your website. If you need any help, use our platform-wise guides."

steps.content: Verify the "Install banner" button on the banner.
steps.expected: The "Install banner" button should be displayed.
The "Install cookie banner on your website" page should be displayed.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify the "80% of pageview limit" warning banner display and actions
cases.preconditions: Website has reached 80% of its monthly pageview limit (set via the test pageview API).

steps.content: Verify the "Heads up! You're at 80% of your pageview limit" banner below the navigation bar.
steps.expected: The banner should be displayed with a warning icon.
"Upgrade now" and "Remind me later" buttons and an "enable extra pageviews" link should be displayed.
The in-app upgrade/pricing page should be displayed.

steps.content: Click the "enable extra pageviews" link in the banner.
steps.expected: The Billing & Invoices page should be displayed. [inferred]
The banner should close.
The banner should reappear only on the next session.
An email with subject "Urgent action required: You've reached 80% of your pageview limit" should be received. [inferred]

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify the "Action required! Your banner will be disabled soon" alert banner display and actions
cases.preconditions: Website has reached its monthly pageview limit and the banner is scheduled for disabling (set via the test pageview/scheduled-task APIs).

steps.content: Verify the "Action required! Your banner will be disabled soon" banner below the navigation bar.
steps.expected: The banner should be displayed with a warning icon.
"Upgrade now" and "Remind me later" buttons and an "enable extra pageviews" link should be displayed.
The in-app upgrade/pricing page should be displayed.

steps.content: Click the "enable extra pageviews" link in the banner.
steps.expected: The Billing & Invoices page should be displayed. [inferred]
The banner should close.
The banner should reappear only on the next session.
An email with subject "Urgent action required: You've reached 95% of your pageview limit" should be received. [inferred]

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify the "Critical alert! Your banner is now disabled" alert banner display and actions
cases.preconditions: Website has exceeded its monthly pageview limit and the banner has been disabled (set via the test pageview/scheduled-task/banner-disable APIs).

steps.content: Verify the "Critical alert! Your banner is now disabled" banner below the navigation bar.
steps.expected: The banner should be displayed with a warning icon.
An "Upgrade now" button and an "enable extra pageviews" link should be displayed (no "Remind me later" — this banner is not dismissible).
The in-app upgrade/pricing page should be displayed.

steps.content: Click the "enable extra pageviews" link in the banner.
steps.expected: The Billing & Invoices page should be displayed. [inferred]

steps.content: Open the live website that has exceeded its pageview limit.
steps.expected: The cookie banner should be disabled and no longer displayed on the website. [inferred]
An email with subject "Your cookie banner is disabled. Take action now" should be received. [inferred]

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify the "Cookie banner missing" compliance alert appears and routes to Cookie Manager
cases.preconditions: Initial cookie scan completed; no active cookie banner on the site.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the "Introducing extra pageviews" promo banner renders and its actions route correctly
cases.preconditions: An active banner is configured on a live website.

steps.content: Observe the top of the Dashboard.
steps.expected: The "Introducing extra pageviews for your sites!" promo banner should be displayed at the top of the Dashboard.

steps.content: Click the "Enable extra pageviews" button in the banner.
steps.expected: The Billing & Invoices page should be displayed.

steps.content: Return to the Dashboard and click the "Learn more" link in the banner.
steps.expected: The pageview overage documentation page should be displayed.

steps.content: Return to the Dashboard and click the close (X) button on the banner.
steps.expected: The promo banner should be dismissed and no longer displayed.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the "You've hit 100% of your pageview limit" banner shows and routes correctly (extra-pageviews auto-enabled path)
cases.preconditions: An active banner is configured on a live website. The existing user has consumed 100% of the plan's pageview limit with extra pageviews enabled.

steps.expected: The "You've hit 100% of your pageview limit" banner should be displayed on the Dashboard.

steps.content: Click the "review usage" link in the banner.
steps.expected: The Reports page should be displayed.

steps.content: Return to the Dashboard and click the "Upgrade now" button in the banner.
steps.expected: The pricing plan page should be displayed.
An email with subject "You've reached 100% of your pageview limit" should be received confirming the banner stays active and extra pageviews now apply.

cases.title: [Dashboard] Verify that the "Your pageview usage is surging" banner shows and routes correctly
cases.preconditions: An active banner is configured on a live website. The existing user has consumed 100% of the plan limit, is using extra pageviews, and has reached ~65% of the next plan tier's pageview limit.

steps.expected: The "Your pageview usage is surging" banner should be displayed on the Dashboard.

steps.content: Click the "review usage" link in the banner.
steps.expected: The Reports page should be displayed.

steps.content: Return to the Dashboard and click the "Upgrade now" button in the banner.
steps.expected: The pricing plan page should be displayed.
An email with subject "Heads up: Your pageview usage continues to grow" should be received.
